text: Use 'el agua' in singular to avoid two strong 'a' sounds, even though agua is feminine.
Write mamá with an accent on the final 'a' when referring to mom.
Use 'en casa' to say someone is at home: 'Estoy en casa'.
Puerta is feminine, so use 'la puerta' in singular.
Use 'en la mesa' for location: 'El pan está en la mesa'.
Use 'esta silla' for this chair near you, and 'esa silla' for one farther away.
Say 'tengo hambre' for 'I am hungry' (literally, 'I have hunger').
Niña uses ñ, which is a separate letter from n in Spanish.
Cocina is feminine, so use 'la cocina'.
Use 'vaso de agua' to say 'glass of water'.
For food in general, Spanish often drops the article: '¿Quieres pan?'.
Use 'un poco de sal' to ask for or describe a small amount of salt.
